words.definition: one of the foods used to make a dish
example_sentences: The main ingredient in hummus is chickpeas.
Check the ingredients list on the label.
I don't have all the ingredients for this recipe.
words.pronunciation_tip: in-GREE-dee-unt — four syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'components of food' — say 'ingredients'.

words.definition: a set of instructions for preparing a dish
example_sentences: This is my grandmother's recipe for apple cake.
I found a simple recipe online and followed it.
Always read the recipe carefully before you start cooking.
words.pronunciation_tip: RES-ih-pee — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'instruction for cooking' — say 'recipe'. The final e is pronounced: RES-ih-pee.
words.collocations: ['follow a recipe', 'traditional recipe', 'try a new recipe']

words.definition: the style of cooking typical of a country or region
example_sentences: Italian cuisine is popular all over the world.
She loves trying different cuisines when she travels.
The restaurant specialises in Moroccan cuisine.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'the food of Italy' in formal contexts — say 'Italian cuisine'.

words.definition: the amount of food served to or eaten by one person
example_sentences: The portions at that restaurant are enormous.
A healthy portion of pasta is about 80 grams dried.
Can I have a smaller portion of chips, please?
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'a piece of food' — say 'a portion' for a serving.

words.definition: salt, pepper, herbs, or spices added to food to improve flavour
example_sentences: The soup needs a little more seasoning.
She rubbed seasoning onto the chicken before roasting.
Taste the food and adjust the seasoning as needed.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I add taste' — say 'I add seasoning' or 'I season the food'.

words.definition: to soak food in a liquid mixture to add flavour or make it tender
example_sentences: Marinate the chicken in lemon juice overnight.
The beef was marinated in red wine and garlic.
You need to marinate it for at least two hours.
words.pronunciation_tip: MAIR-ih-nayt — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I put the meat in sauce for a long time' — say 'I marinate the meat'.

words.definition: how food feels in your mouth
example_sentences: The texture of the ice cream was perfectly smooth.
I don't like the soft texture of overcooked vegetables.
The dish had a lovely creamy, velvety texture.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'how the food feels' — say 'the texture of the food'.

words.definition: containing substances your body needs to stay healthy
example_sentences: A salad with protein and vegetables is very nutritious.
It's important to eat nutritious meals every day.
This smoothie is almost as nutritious as a full meal.
words.pronunciation_tip: nyoo-TRISH-us — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'food that is healthy' — say 'nutritious food' or 'a nutritious meal'.

words.definition: the feeling of wanting to eat
example_sentences: Exercise always gives me a good appetite.
She completely lost her appetite when she was stressed.
The smell of fresh bread really increases the appetite.
words.pronunciation_tip: AP-ih-tyt — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I don't want to eat' — say 'I have no appetite' or 'I've lost my appetite'.

words.definition: having a medical reaction to a particular food or substance
example_sentences: I'm allergic to nuts — even a small amount is dangerous.
She has an allergic reaction to all dairy products.
Are you allergic to anything I should know about?
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I can't eat nuts because of my health' — say 'I'm allergic to nuts'.

words.definition: having a salty or spicy taste rather than sweet
example_sentences: I prefer savoury snacks to sweet ones at parties.
This is a savoury pie filled with cheese and vegetables.
Hummus has a wonderful savoury, nutty flavour.
words.pronunciation_tip: SAY-vuh-ree — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'not sweet food' — say 'savoury food'.

words.definition: having very little taste or flavour
example_sentences: This soup is completely bland — it desperately needs salt.
The diet food tasted bland and completely uninteresting.
He prefers bland food because spicy things hurt his stomach.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'food without taste' — say 'bland food'.

words.definition: to have a very strong desire for a particular food
example_sentences: I always crave chocolate when I'm feeling stressed.
She craved salty food throughout her pregnancy.
Do you ever crave food in the middle of the night?
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I really want to eat something' — say 'I crave it' or 'I have a craving'.

words.definition: to allow yourself to enjoy something you normally avoid
example_sentences: She occasionally indulges in a piece of chocolate cake.
We indulged in an enormous seafood dinner to celebrate.
It's perfectly fine to indulge once in a while.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I allow myself to eat bad things' — say 'I indulge occasionally'.

words.definition: good for your health and made from simple, natural foods
example_sentences: She prefers wholesome home-cooked meals to takeaways.
The restaurant serves simple, wholesome, honest food.
A wholesome diet based on vegetables and wholegrains is ideal.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'natural and healthy food' every time — say 'wholesome food'.

words.definition: to cook food slowly in liquid just below boiling point
example_sentences: Let the soup simmer for twenty minutes on a low heat.
Simmer the sauce very gently — don't let it boil.
She left the stew simmering all afternoon.
words.common_mistake: Don't confuse with 'boil' — simmer is gentler and slower.
words.collocations: ['simmer for', 'simmer on low heat', 'leave to simmer']

words.definition: a small amount of food used to decorate a dish
example_sentences: The chef added a fresh parsley garnish to the soup.
Garnish the plate with a thin slice of lemon.
The dessert was garnished with a sprig of mint.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'decoration on food' — say 'garnish'.

words.definition: something used in place of another ingredient
example_sentences: I used yoghurt as a substitute for cream in the sauce.
You can substitute honey for sugar in most recipes.
Low-fat cheese makes a good substitute here.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I used something instead' — say 'I used X as a substitute for Y'.

words.definition: to eat small amounts of food by taking small bites
example_sentences: She was nibbling on crackers while she worked.
Don't nibble all afternoon or you won't eat dinner.
We had some nibbles with drinks before the main course.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I eat a little bit' — say 'I nibble on something'.

words.definition: managing how much you eat to maintain a healthy diet
example_sentences: Portion control is one of the keys to a healthy weight.
She practises portion control by using smaller plates.
Good portion control means eating the right amount.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I control how much I eat' — say 'I practise portion control'.
words.collocations: ['practise portion control', 'portion control tips', 'importance of portion control']
